what_it_does: Implements a file-discovered registry of PlatformEntry rows: it scans directories of platforms/<id>.py files, loads each module, validates its module-level PLATFORM dict through PlatformEntry.model_validate, optionally re-derives transport invariants from a registered engine thunk, and exposes the loaded platforms through a dict-like PlatformRegistry interface plus a factory function platform_registry().
observations: Uses importlib.util.spec_from_file_location and exec_module to load platform modules dynamically.
Skips non-.py files and files starting with '_' during directory discovery.
Requires each PLATFORM dict to validate against the PlatformEntry Pydantic model and raises on failure.
Enforces that the declared platform id matches the file name after normalizing hyphens and underscores.
Provides rediscover() which clears and reloads so removed files unregister.
Implements __getitem__, __contains__, __iter__, __len__, get, and ids() to act as a dict-like platform table.
_derive_invariants lazily imports introspection.engine and, if a head builder is registered, overwrites signal_sets.transport_invariants with a derived set.
platform_registry() returns a fresh registry on each call rather than a cached singleton.
standouts: F-FIX-2: transport_invariants are overwritten at load by values derived from a live engine thunk when one is registered.
PG-D5: full nested-tree validation via PlatformEntry.model_validate with loud failure on malformed rows.
Addressable-by-file discipline: platform id must match the module name modulo hyphen/underscore equivalence.
Standalone copy of the importlib registry pattern with no shared base class.
conventions: Follows the 'standalone copy' (S3) discipline: rewrites the importlib discovery loop locally instead of inheriting a base registry.
Fail-loud: malformed PLATFORM dicts raise Pydantic ValidationError rather than being skipped.
Module-level sentinel pattern: a file must declare PLATFORM to be considered a platform row.
Fresh factory for cheap discovery, contrasting with expensive cached registries.
concerns: _derive_invariants reaches into engine._HEAD_BUILDERS, a private structure in another module.
exec_module on files in the platforms directory executes arbitrary code; there is no visible sandboxing or trust check.
register() overwrites self.platforms[entry.id] silently if duplicate ids occur.
_load_module uses a synthetic module name _platform_{name} which could collide across directories if multiple dirs contain the same basename.
_derive_invariants performs a lazy import on every call rather than caching the engine reference.
notes: This file is the Level-2 platform table: a self-registering, validated, dict-like registry whose ground truth is the filesystem plus an optional live derivation of transport invariants.
questions: What is the exact schema of PlatformEntry and its nested sub-models?
What does engine.derived_transport_invariants do and what inputs does it require?
How and when are head builders registered in engine._HEAD_BUILDERS?
Is there any concurrency control expected for discover/rediscover?
How are platform files authenticated or sandboxed before exec_module?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it discovers, validates, and exposes platform rows from files and supports live invariant derivation.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the platform catalog self-maintaining from filesystem declarations while keeping the registry authoritative through strict validation and optional live derivation of transport invariants.
novelty: It deliberately re-implements the importlib discovery loop locally per S3 rather than using a shared base registry, and it overwrites declared transport_invariants with engine-derived values at load time.
contribution: Provides a validated, dict-like, file-discovered platform registry with id/filename consistency checks and a hook for live derivation of transport invariants, usable anywhere a platform table is needed.
summary_for_embedding: A Pydantic-validated, importlib-based, dict-like registry that loads platform rows from platforms/<id>.py files and optionally derives transport invariants from a registered engine thunk.
intention_for_embedding: To create a self-registering, authoritative platform table whose filesystem declarations are loudly validated and whose runtime invariants can be derived from live builder templates.

symbols.does: Normalizes a platform id or module name by lowercasing and replacing hyphens with underscores.
symbols.description: Used to compare the declared PLATFORM id against the file name so that hyphenated canonical ids and underscore-containing Python module names are treated as equivalent.

symbols.does: Loads a platforms/<id>.py file as a Python module using importlib.
symbols.description: Creates a module from a file path with a synthetic name, executes it, and returns the base name and module object; this is the local importlib discovery primitive.

symbols.does: Class representing the file-discovered, dict-like platform registry.
symbols.description: Encapsulates the set of validated PlatformEntry rows, the directories to scan, and methods for discovery, registration, rediscovery, and dict-like access.

symbols.does: Initializes an empty platform registry with no directories.
symbols.description: Sets up the internal platforms dictionary and the list of discovery directories.

symbols.does: Scans given directories and registers every valid platforms/<id>.py file.
symbols.description: Iterates sorted directory entries, skips non-.py and underscore-prefixed files, loads each module, and calls register() for any module declaring a PLATFORM dict.

symbols.does: Clears the registry and re-runs discovery.
symbols.description: Ensures that removed platform files are unregistered and new or changed files are reloaded from the filesystem.

symbols.does: Validates and registers a PLATFORM dict under a given module name.
symbols.description: Validates the dict as a PlatformEntry, checks that the entry id matches the file name modulo hyphen/underscore normalization, derives invariants if a head builder is wired, and stores the entry.

symbols.does: Replaces transport_invariants with engine-derived values when a head builder is registered.
symbols.description: Lazily imports introspection.engine and, if a thunk exists for the platform id, computes derived transport invariants and returns a copy of the entry with the updated signal_sets.

symbols.does: Returns the PlatformEntry for a given platform id.
symbols.description: Dict-like read access to a registered platform row.

symbols.does: Checks whether a platform id is registered.
symbols.description: Dict-like membership test against the internal platforms dictionary.
Allows the registry to be iterated like a dictionary over its keys.

symbols.does: Returns the number of registered platforms.
symbols.description: Dict-like size reporting for the registry.

symbols.does: Returns a PlatformEntry or a default for a given platform id.
symbols.description: Dict-like safe lookup that returns the supplied default if the id is absent.

symbols.does: Returns a sorted list of registered platform ids.
symbols.description: Convenience accessor for the ordered set of platform keys.

symbols.does: Factory that creates and discovers a fresh PlatformRegistry.
symbols.description: Builds a PlatformRegistry over the default platforms directory or caller-supplied directories and immediately runs discovery; returns a new instance each call.
